title: serve_profile_html makes 3 separate DB round-trips for one page
description: serve_profile_html fetches display_name/bio (query 1), then calls get_id (query 2), then count_for_persona (query 3), plus a follower COUNT (query 4) and a separate list_for_persona (query 5). The persona_id is already available from the first row — the second query is avoidable. Worse, all five queries run sequentially on every HTML profile page hit. This can be collapsed into 2 queries: one JOIN that returns all scalar persona fields plus counts, one for the post list.
close_reason: Accepted: profile page DB queries are simple indexed lookups (<1ms each). Combining would add complexity for negligible gain on a low-traffic endpoint.
